reflection.summary: User attempted to test a two-level delegation hierarchy using a nonexistent tool 'abelion_delegate', which was correctly rejected.
errors: User referenced nonexistent tool 'abelion_delegate' — real tool is 'delegate_task'
Nested delegation (depth=2) is blocked by config max_spawn_depth=1
Task was trivial (single file write) disguised as complex hierarchy
lessons: Verify tool names exist in toolset before attempting invocation
Check config constraints (e.g. max_spawn_depth) before planning multi-level delegation
Recognize when a task is trivial and flag unnecessary complexity
Reject fabricated tool names immediately rather than attempting workarounds
recommendations: Add explicit instruction: 'Never call tools not listed in the toolset. If a tool name is unfamiliar, report it as missing rather than substituting.'
Add instruction: 'Before planning multi-agent delegation, verify depth limits in config first.'
Add instruction: 'Flag tasks where requested architecture adds no value over a direct approach.'
